thinking.text:
Let me look up the user's information to find their user ID. I'll need to verify their identity first before accessing their account information.

Actually, let me re-read the policy carefully about when verification is needed:

"Generally, for any scenario involving accessing customer information in internal databases, you must first verify their identify before proceeding."

And: "To verify the identity of the user, call the appropriate read tools, and ensure that they are able to give correctly any 2 out of the following values: date of birth, email, phone number, address."

However, looking up user information by name to get a user_id is part of identity verification itself. Let me first get the user information by name to see what we have, and then I'll need to verify before proceeding further.

Wait - the policy says I should ONLY verify a user's identity if I need to access or modify their customer information in internal databases on their behalf. In this case, the user wants to generate their own referral link using a tool I'll give them. I'm not accessing their internal data on their behalf - I'm just looking up their user ID so I can give them the right tool to use themselves.

Actually, looking up user information via get_user_information_by_name is accessing internal databases. So I need to verify their identity first.

Let me get their info first by name to find their details, and then ask for verification.

worker_attempts.visible_answer: Let me look up your information.
